heading: Plans to address needs of families of passengers involved in foreign air carrier accidents
label: 41313 Plans to address needs of families of passengers involved in foreign air carrier accidents

children.heading: .—Aircraft accident
children.label: 1 .—Aircraft accident
children.text: The term “aircraft accident” means any aviation disaster, regardless of its cause or suspected cause, that occurs within the United States; and

children.heading: .—Passenger
children.label: 2 .—Passenger
children.text: The term “passenger” has the meaning given such term by section 1136.

children.heading: .—Submission of Plans
children.label: b .—Submission of Plans
children.text: A foreign air carrier providing foreign air transportation under this chapter shall transmit to the Secretary of Transportation and the Chairman of the National Transportation Safety Board a plan for addressing the needs of the families of passengers involved in an aircraft accident that involves an aircraft under the control of the foreign air carrier and results in any loss of life.

children.heading: .—Telephone number
children.label: 1 .—Telephone number
children.text: A plan for publicizing a reliable, toll-free telephone number and staff to take calls to such number from families of passengers involved in an aircraft accident that involves an aircraft under the control of the foreign air carrier and results in any loss of life.

children.label: A
children.text: the organization designated for the accident under section 1136(a)(2); or

children.label: B
children.text: other suitably trained individuals.

children.heading: .—Notice provided as soon as possible
children.label: 3 .—Notice provided as soon as possible
children.text: An assurance that the notice required by paragraph (2) shall be provided as soon as practicable after the foreign air carrier has verified the identity of a passenger on the foreign aircraft, whether or not the names of all of the passengers have been verified.

children.label: A
children.text: the director of family support services designated for the accident under section 1136(a)(1); and

children.label: B
children.text: the organization designated for the accident under section 1136(a)(2).

children.heading: .—Consultation regarding disposition of remains and effects
children.label: 5 .—Consultation regarding disposition of remains and effects
children.text: An assurance that the family of each passenger will be consulted about the disposition of any remains and personal effects of the passenger that are within the control of the foreign air carrier.

children.heading: .—Return of possessions
children.label: 6 .—Return of possessions
children.text: An assurance that, if requested by the family of a passenger, any possession (regardless of its condition) of that passenger that is within the control of the foreign air carrier will be returned to the family unless the possession is needed for the accident investigation or a criminal investigation.

children.heading: .—Unclaimed possessions retained
children.label: 7 .—Unclaimed possessions retained
children.text: An assurance that any unclaimed possession of a passenger within the control of the foreign air carrier will be retained by the foreign air carrier for not less than 18 months after the date of the accident.

children.heading: .—Monuments
children.label: 8 .—Monuments
children.text: An assurance that the family of each passenger will be consulted about construction by the foreign air carrier of any monument to the passengers built in the United States, including any inscription on the monument.

children.heading: .—Equal treatment of passengers
children.label: 9 .—Equal treatment of passengers
children.text: An assurance that the treatment of the families of nonrevenue passengers (and any other victim of the accident, including any victim on the ground) will be the same as the treatment of the families of revenue passengers.

children.heading: .—Service and assistance to families of passengers
children.label: 10 .—Service and assistance to families of passengers
children.text: An assurance that the foreign air carrier will work with any organization designated under section 1136(a)(2) on an ongoing basis to ensure that families of passengers receive an appropriate level of services and assistance following an accident.

children.heading: .—Compensation to service organizations
children.label: 11 .—Compensation to service organizations
children.text: An assurance that the foreign air carrier will provide reasonable compensation to any organization designated under section 1136(a)(2) for services and assistance provided by the organization.

children.heading: .—Travel and care expenses
children.label: 12 .—Travel and care expenses
children.text: An assurance that the foreign air carrier will assist the family of any passenger in traveling to the location of the accident and provide for the physical care of the family while the family is staying at such location.

children.heading: .—Resources for plan
children.label: 13 .—Resources for plan
children.text: An assurance that the foreign air carrier will commit sufficient resources to carry out the plan.

children.heading: .—Substitute measures
children.label: 14 .—Substitute measures
children.text: If a foreign air carrier does not wish to comply with paragraph (10), (11), or (12), a description of proposed adequate substitute measures for the requirements of each paragraph with which the foreign air carrier does not wish to comply.

children.heading: .—Training of employees and agents
children.label: 15 .—Training of employees and agents
children.text: An assurance that the foreign air carrier will provide adequate training to the employees and agents of the carrier to meet the needs of survivors and family members following an accident.

children.heading: .—Consultation on carrier response not covered by plan
children.label: 16 .—Consultation on carrier response not covered by plan
children.text: An assurance that, in the event that the foreign air carrier volunteers assistance to United States citizens within the United States with respect to an aircraft accident outside the United States involving any loss of life, the foreign air carrier shall consult with the Board and the Department of State on the provision of the assistance.

children.heading: Notice concerning liability for manmade structures.—
children.label: 17 Notice concerning liability for manmade structures.—

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: A .—In general
children.text: An assurance that, in the case of an accident that results in any damage to a manmade structure or other property on the ground that is not government-owned, the foreign air carrier will promptly provide notice, in writing, to the extent practicable, directly to the owner of the structure or other property about liability for any property damage and means for obtaining compensation.

children.heading: .—Minimum contents
children.label: B .—Minimum contents
children.text: At a minimum, the written notice shall advise an owner (i) to contact the insurer of the property as the authoritative source for information about coverage and compensation; (ii) to not rely on unofficial information offered by foreign air carrier representatives about compensation by the foreign air carrier for accident-site property damage; and (iii) to obtain photographic or other detailed evidence of property damage as soon as possible after the accident, consistent with restrictions on access to the accident site.

children.heading: .—Simultaneous electronic transmission of ntsb hearing
children.label: 18 .—Simultaneous electronic transmission of ntsb hearing
children.text: An assurance that, in the case of an accident in which the National Transportation Safety Board conducts a public hearing or comparable proceeding at a location greater than 80 miles from the accident site, the foreign air carrier will ensure that the proceeding is made available simultaneously by electronic means at a location open to the public at both the origin city and destination city of the foreign air carrier’s flight if that city is located in the United States.

children.heading: .—Permit and Exemption Requirement
children.label: d .—Permit and Exemption Requirement
children.text: The Secretary shall not approve an application for a permit under section 41302 unless the applicant has included as part of the application or request for exemption a plan that meets the requirements of subsection (c).

children.heading: .—Limitation on Liability
children.label: e .—Limitation on Liability
children.text: A foreign air carrier shall not be liable for damages in any action brought in a Federal or State court arising out of the performance of the foreign air carrier in preparing or providing a passenger list pursuant to a plan submitted by the foreign air carrier under subsection (c), unless the liability was caused by conduct of the foreign air carrier which was grossly negligent or which constituted intentional misconduct.
